mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods with declining FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 6.5% indicates labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weak demand, causing S&P 500 declines over 60 trading days via reduced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with simultaneous GDPC1 and HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.0 signals stronger supply chain activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 102.0.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 2.5% triggers inflation fears, prompting central bank tightening and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with CPIAUCSL above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer demand, driving S&P 500 gains over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods with simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust labor market health, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days via sustained consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100.5 combined with falling GDP (GDPC1) indicates supply-side contraction, causing S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with simultaneous INDPRO and GDPC1 declines.
